summary: Full-stack LevelUp MVP testing. Backend: 22/22 pytest passing (profile, tasks, goals, achievements, stats, seed, reset). Frontend: Home, Quests (create+complete+delete), Goals (create+progress), Progress (achievements+weekly chart), Profile (edit name + reset) all working on mobile viewport 390x844. Focus Mode timer starts and counts down correctly. One critical web-specific issue: Alert.alert with 3 buttons (Exit Early challenge selection) does not render any visible dialog on React Native Web, blocking the pushup/breathing challenge flow on web preview.

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking 'Exit Early (Challenge)' during a running timer invokes Alert.alert with 3 buttons (Keep Focusing / 20 Push-ups / 4 Breath Cycles). On React Native Web this multi-button Alert renders nothing visible and no window.dialog fires, so users cannot pick a challenge, and the pushup-tap / breath-orb screens are unreachable via UI on web. Works on native but blocks web testing & web users.

react_native_issues.issue: Deprecation warnings in console: 'shadow* style props are deprecated. Use boxShadow' and 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Non-blocking but should be cleaned up.
Expo warns: react-native-svg@15.15.4 installed but expected 15.12.1 for installed Expo version. SVG charts render fine, but version mismatch should be addressed via 'npx expo install --check'.

action_items: Replace Alert.alert in app/focus.tsx tryExit() with a custom React Native Modal that shows 'Keep Focusing / 20 Push-ups / 4 Breath Cycles' option buttons. This will fix the broken web flow AND work on native.
Run 'npx expo install --check' inside /app/frontend and accept react-native-svg downgrade to eliminate version warning.
Optional: migrate remaining shadow* style props to boxShadow and pointerEvents prop to style.pointerEvents to silence deprecation warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is single-file ~585 lines - acceptable but getting close to 700-line guideline; consider splitting routers.
MongoDB _id is correctly excluded in all responses (uses {"_id": 0} projections) - good.
All env vars sourced correctly (MONGO_URL, DB_NAME, EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL).
Input validation via Pydantic Literal types for focus_area/time_slot - good.
Proper async/await throughout backend; streak + achievement logic is idempotent per day.
Level-up detection uses prev_level snapshot before $inc - correct pattern.
xp clamped to 0 in uncomplete_task - good defensive handling.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (all tabs + all CRUD work; only Focus challenge selection blocked on web)

seed_data_creation: Backend: POST /api/profile/reset + POST /api/seed invoked by pytest fixture. After UI tests, DB holds 1 TEST_UI_Task custom task, 1 TEST_UI_Goal, and completion logs. Safe to re-reset via /api/profile/reset before next run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_levelup.py is stable and fast (~3s). Run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_levelup.py -v. Frontend Focus Mode challenge flow cannot be tested on web until main agent replaces Alert.alert with a custom Modal. Once fixed, verify: start timer -> escape -> pick pushup -> tap 20 times -> success, and start timer -> escape -> pick breathing -> wait 4 cycles.
rca_of_issue: Focus Mode Alert RCA: React Native Web's Alert.alert has limited support for multi-button alerts (>2 buttons). While RNWeb routes single/two-button alerts through window.confirm, three-button alerts are silently no-op on many versions. Reproduction: open /focus -> tap 'Start Focus Session' -> tap 'Exit Early (Challenge)' -> observe nothing renders and timer keeps running. Mitigation: swap Alert.alert for a custom <Modal visible={showExit}> with three <TouchableOpacity> buttons that set challenge state. Minimal change localized to tryExit() and component render in app/focus.tsx.
